one_line_ask: Take a licensed Adobe Stock business photograph plus three client icon sketches and produce a cohesive VC-website graphics set: a brand-graded full-bleed hero, responsive desktop/tablet/mobile section crops, a tinted section-divider variant, and clean SVG icons — all chained from the licensed source.
brief: Our venture-capital firm's website works structurally, but the graphics feel dated and no longer signal the credibility we want. Refresh the visual layer into a cohesive, web-ready set that matches our brand. (1) HERO: source a high-quality, forward-thinking business/finance photograph from Adobe Stock (modern glass-and-light office or abstract data-light scene, no recognizable faces or logos), license it at full resolution, then color-grade it to our brand palette — primary deep navy #0B1F3A, slate #2E3A4D, warm gold #C8A24B accent: auto-tone for a clean baseline, cool the white balance toward navy, pull the HSL so blues read as our navy and the gold accent stays warm, and lay a subtle navy color-overlay so the hero reads on-brand rather than generic. (2) FULL-BLEED: the licensed crop isn't wide enough for our 2560px hero band, so outpaint/extend the graded image to a 2560x1080 (≈21:9) full-bleed canvas using generative expand, keeping the new edges photographically consistent. (3) RESPONSIVE: from that master hero, produce the three breakpoint crops the brief requires — desktop 2560x1080, tablet 1536x1024 (4:3-ish), and mobile 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait) — each subject-aware so the
